Kidney.name: Kidney
Kidney.function: A pair of bean-shaped organs that filter your entire blood supply around 30 times a day, clearing waste and excess water to make urine. They also balance salts, control blood pressure and keep your body's chemistry steady.

Renal pelvis.name: Renal Pelvis
Renal pelvis.function: The funnel-shaped collecting space inside each kidney where freshly made urine gathers. It channels the urine out of the kidney and down the ureter towards the bladder.

Suprarenal gland.name: Suprarenal (Adrenal) Gland
Suprarenal gland.function: A small hormone gland perched on top of each kidney. It releases adrenaline for the fight-or-flight response and cortisol and aldosterone to manage stress, salt balance and blood pressure.

Urinary bladder.name: Urinary Bladder
Urinary bladder.function: A stretchy muscular pouch that stores urine delivered by the ureters until you are ready to go. When it empties, its wall squeezes to push urine out through the urethra.
